features.summary: Broadcom reported Q3 FY2021 consolidated revenue of $6.8 billion, up 16% year-over-year, with semiconductor solutions growing 19% to $5 billion and infrastructure software growing 10% to $1.8 billion. The company maintained strong demand from hypercloud and service provider customers while wireless had strong year-over-year comparisons. Enterprise spending showed signs of recovery but remained down year-over-year in Q3, though strong bookings suggest improvement ahead.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q4 consolidated revenue of $7.35 billion, up 14% year-over-year, with continued double-digit growth momentum. They anticipate enterprise recovery to be a key growth driver in Q4 and into 2022, with server storage connectivity expected to grow low double-digits year-over-year in Q4 after declining 9% in Q3. The company sees sustained demand from cloud and service providers rather than a rollover, while wireless is expected to ramp 33% sequentially in Q4 supporting next-generation smartphone launches.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints continue to be a significant challenge, though the company maintains stable lead times and has secured capacity for 2022. COVID-19 resurgence in Malaysia, where Broadcom has significant supply chain operations, poses operational risks despite 99% employee vaccination rates. The company faces potential foundry price increases and must carefully manage inventory to avoid building excess buffers in the supply chain during this constrained environment.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand is driven by cloud customers upgrading to next-generation 800 gigabit Tomahawk 4 and Trident switches, with Broadcom being the only provider of 25.6 terabit switches and 100G SerDes technology. Broadband growth of 23% year-over-year is fueled by 2x growth in Wi-Fi 6 gateway deployments and multi-billion dollar fiber investments by major telecom operators like AT&T, British Telecom, and Deutsche Telekom. The company maintains leadership positions in specialized silicon engines for hyperscale customers and benefits from aggressive migration to 18TB hard disk drives in cloud environments.
